ps140: 1, Podoben “The joy of the heavenly ranks…”

Clothed in the garments of truth whiter than snow,* let us rejoice in this Paschal day,* in which Christ, as the Sun of the truth, rose from the dead,* and make us all radiant** with the splendor of incorruption.

This day is the king and lord,* the feast of feasts,* the day which the Lord truly made,* in which, as David sings, let us rejoice, O people,* as despite the closed door,** He granted to his disciples peace.

The unbelief of Thomas* makes us believe in the Resurrection of the Lord Word from the oubliette of Hades,* that brings salvation to the world.* As Thomas confidently touches the wounds of the Hands and Feet* to bring the faith to the world** with his curious hand.

When the doors were shut,* Jesus stood in the midst of His disciples.* He granted them courage and peace.* Then He spoke to the apostle Thomas:* Do you not believe that I am risen from the dead?* Bring your hands here, and touch My side, and see!* Because of your unbelief, everyone learned of My Passion and My Resurrection,* so that they can join your cry:* My Lord and my God, glory to You!
aposticha: Touch my side with your hand, O Thomas.* Come and feel the scars of the nails.* Know in faith and do not be faithless, but believing!* Thomas, when he touched the Master, cried:* You are my God and Lord,* O compassionate One, glory to You!
